zh.Shavington cum Gresty, Cheshire, England: 英國英格蘭爾沙文頓 姆 格雷斯迪
zh.lifeSummary: 當Eva Brow於大約1340年出生在英國英格蘭爾沙文頓 姆 格雷斯迪時，她的父親John Brow27歲，母親Mrs. John Brow22歲。她於大約1369年與Randall Woodward在英國爾沙文頓 姆 格雷斯迪結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世在她的家鄉。

nameMeanings.name: Brow
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: either a descriptive nickname for someone with bushy or otherwise distinctive eyebrows, from Middle English broue ‘eyebrow, eyelid, forehead’ (Old English brū), but, more likely, a topographic name for someone who lived at the brow of a hill from a transferred use of the same word; surnames of the type de la Browe are recorded from the end of the 13th century.
Altered form of French   Brault   and also of American French   Breaux  . Compare   Bro   and   Broe  .

Americanized form of Dutch Brouw: perhaps a nominal variant of Op den Brouw, a habitational name for someone living at the ‘Brouw’, a toponym of unclear origin, or just a place for beer brewing (Brouw being a shortened form of brouwen ‘to brew’ or brouwer ‘brewer’).

variants.name: Brault
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Adelard, Alain, Andre, Gilles, Rosaire, Solange, Cecile, Emile, Francoise, Germaine, Girard.
French: variant of   Braud  . Its most common American French form is   Breaux  ; it was Americanized (Anglicized) as   Brow  ,   Broe  , and   Bro  . Compare   Breault  .
Altered form of American French   Breaux   back to its original form.

nameMeanings.name: Eva
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Popular Latinate form of   Eve  , used also as an Anglicization of the Irish name Aoife .

en.lifeSummary: When Eva Brow was born about 1340, in Shavington cum Gresty, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, John Brow, was 27 and her mother, Mrs. John Brow, was 22. She married Randall Woodward about 1369, in Shavington cum Gresty, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in her hometown.
